legacy_recognition_rule: Select the monthly annuity performance workbook and preserve source row identity from the annuity sheet.
validation_check: Batch id must equal annuity_performance:<period> and anchor row 2 must survive into the first intake record.
ambiguity_notes: Replay intake receives an explicit workbook path, so directory discovery parity remains an open Phase 1 ambiguity. The real workbook sample .planning/phases/01-legacy-capability-mapping-parity-harness/artifacts/runtime/phase1-parity-offline-2026-04-12-run-001/【年金规模收入】26年2月年金业务规模明细(手工汇总)_sample.xlsx is tracked as supplemental contract-gap evidence and does not replace the must-pass deep-sample replay input.

legacy_recognition_rule: Merge trustee and investee sheets into one stable intake stream while preserving per-sheet source metadata.
validation_check: Merged annual award intake must produce two records with anchor rows [2, 3] and source_sheet values TrusteeAwards and InvesteeAwards.
ambiguity_notes: Phase 1 maps the merged-sheet intake contract explicitly even though legacy discovery and operator flows are still broader than replay intake.

legacy_recognition_rule: Merge trustee and investee loss sheets into one stable intake stream while preserving customer-name payloads.
validation_check: Merged annual loss intake must produce two records with anchor rows [2, 3] and preserve the two expected customer names across both source sheets.
ambiguity_notes: The contract is explicit in Pro and already tested, but legacy operator discovery remains outside this replay-only intake slice.

legacy_recognition_rule: Ignore trailing empty rows so loss intake does not invent empty records from workbook padding.
validation_check: Trailing all-null and all-empty rows must be skipped so the batch row count remains 2 for the fixture workbook.
ambiguity_notes: This validation check is phase-critical because empty-row drift would change source-recognition counts before later parity comparison.
